title: Microsoft fire idTech team at Id software
titleZh: 微软解雇了 Id software 的 idTech 团队
author: bauc
relevanceReason: 帖子报道了微软对Xbox部门进行大规模重组，导致id Software的idTech引擎开发团队被裁撤。idTech是游戏行业的重要技术引擎，此事件涉及科技巨头微软的战略调整及其对游戏开发技术生态的影响，属于科技产品与行业动态范畴。
summaryRaw: id Software are without a doubt one of the most important game developers in the first person genre history. The technology empowering their games, idTech powers a shocking number of games and even game engines ( we ranked it 4th in our Most Important Game Engines of all Time ranking). This era might be coming to a close as it’s being reported that most, if not all, developers working on idTech at id Software have been fired.

This comes as part of the MASSIVE layoffs across all Xbox divisions. Asha Sharma, the new CEO of Xbox tweeted the email that was sent to the entire company. It explains why the layoffs are happening, starting with:

We are beginning the most significant restructure in XBOX history. After careful consideration, I’ve made the difficult decision to reduce our team by approximately 3,200 throughout FY27. This will include approximately 1,600 role eliminations today, and in addition, four studios will leave XBOX to new management. I recognize that a year-long restructuring creates additional challenges. Unfortunately, it is not possible to make all the necessary changes in a single day, and I wanted to be direct about the scale.

Since this announcement, several additional details have been made available about the impact of the layoffs, several surrounding id Software, starting with this Tweet from Apogee founder Scott Miller:
Additionally Rebs Gaming tweeted the following linked in post from impacted id Software developer and 20+ year id veteran Michael Maynard:
summaryZh: 微软对Xbox部门进行大规模重组，裁减约3200个岗位，涉及id Software的idTech引擎开发团队。
idTech是游戏行业的重要技术引擎，此次裁撤可能影响其未来发展和游戏生态。
此次裁员是微软Xbox历史上最大规模的重组，部分工作室将脱离Xbox管理。
highlightsZh: id Software作为FPS游戏领域的先驱，其idTech引擎对游戏行业影响深远。
微软Xbox CEO Asha Sharma宣布裁员计划，涉及约1600个岗位立即裁撤。
idTech引擎曾支撑大量游戏及引擎开发，此次裁撤可能改变游戏技术格局。
裁员引发行业关注，多位游戏行业资深人士发声讨论其影响。

comments.author: ndiddy
comments.textRawHtml: I think we'll see stuff like this continue to happen over time. As a game company, having your own engine means that you have to be able to cultivate internal expertise in your tooling. Your employees will know this and could do bad things like ask for more money because they know that replacing them would significantly hurt productivity. Meanwhile, laying off your whole engine team and switching to UE5 means that you can get access to tons of low-wage contractors who know UE5. You can hire a bunch of them when you start a game project and then lay them all off when it's finished, and rinse and repeat as necessary. It lets you treat your employees as a replaceable commodity that can be scaled up and down as it makes monetary sense rather than a cohesive team of skilled artisans.
comments.textZhHtml: 我认为随着时间的推移，我们会看到类似的事情继续发生。作为一家游戏公司，拥有自己的引擎意味着您必须能够培养工具方面的内部专业知识。您的员工会知道这一点，并且可能会做坏事，例如索要更多钱，因为他们知道更换他们会严重损害生产力。同时，解雇整个引擎团队并切换到 UE5 意味着您可以接触到大量了解 UE5 的低薪承包商。当你开始一个游戏项目时，你可以雇佣一群人，然后在项目完成后将他们全部解雇，并根据需要进行冲洗和重复。它可以让您将您的员工视为一种可替换的商品，可以根据金钱的需要而扩大或缩小规模，而不是一个有凝聚力的熟练工匠团队。

children.author: munificent
children.textRawHtml:
All of this is true and has been true for decades in the game industry.

The other side of this seesaw is: Games are fundamentally in the novelty business. Players like some amount of familiarity, but they want new experiences. Every game engine has a sort of "grain" to it where it tends to produce games with a certain look and feel. The flat-ish shading and floaty physics of Unity is a particularly visible example of this. So using a widely used game engine can put you at a disadvantage if you're trying to make a game that doesn't go with that grain and offers players something different.

As more studios consolidate on the same engine, more players will get tired of that sameness and reward other studios more. As more studios do their own thing, players will become saturated with novelty and the benefits of not using an engine will go down. There is no stable equilibrium.
children.textZhHtml:
所有这些都是事实，并且在游戏行业几十年来一直如此。

这个跷跷板的另一面是：游戏从根本上来说是一种新奇行业。玩家喜欢一定程度的熟悉感，但他们想要新的体验。每个游戏引擎都有一种“颗粒”，它往往会产生具有特定外观和感觉的游戏。 Unity 的扁平化着色和浮动物理就是一个特别明显的例子。因此，如果您试图制作一款不符合这种要求并为玩家提供不同东西的游戏，那么使用广泛使用的游戏引擎可能会让您处于不利地位。

随着越来越多的工作室整合到同一个引擎上，越来越多的玩家会厌倦这种千篇一律的做法，并向其他工作室提供更多奖励。随着越来越多的工作室做自己的事情，玩家将被新奇事物所饱和，而不使用引擎的好处将会下降。不存在稳定平衡。

children.author: Cpoll
children.textRawHtml:
> Every game engine has a sort of "grain" to it where it tends to produce games with a certain look and feel.

I think this is a bit of a myth. Unreal gets this criticism a lot, but it's usually because many studios choose to stick close to the rendering defaults, which does lead to a certain look.

To that point, it's probably a lot cheaper to configure Unreal or Unity into a unique "grain" than it is to develop your own engine. It's also possible to use custom physics instead of those built into the engine.
children.textZhHtml:
> 每个游戏引擎都有一种“颗粒”，它往往会产生具有特定外观和感觉的游戏。

我认为这有点神话。虚幻经常受到这种批评，但这通常是因为许多工作室选择坚持渲染默认值，这确实导致了某种外观。

就这一点而言，将虚幻或 Unity 配置为独特的“颗粒”可能比开发自己的引擎便宜得多。还可以使用自定义物理场而不是引擎中内置的物理场。

children.author: willis936
children.textRawHtml: I can tell you that the fact that id tech 6 engine games look as good as they do and pump out 200+ fps is pretty novel in a way that the massive epic games and their flagship UE5 (and no painstakingly optimized case studies one may conjure) come close to matching even a decade on.  The reward for lapping the entire industry?  Early retirement.
children.textZhHtml: 我可以告诉你，id tech 6 引擎游戏看起来和它们一样好，并且输出 200+ fps，这在某种程度上是相当新颖的，大型史诗游戏和他们的旗舰 UE5（人们可能不会想到任何精心优化的案例研究）甚至在十年后都接近匹配。  包揽整个行业的回报是什么？  提前退休。

children.author: ahartmetz
children.textRawHtml: I have also been very impressed by the performance and fluidity of Doom and Doom Eternal. Even when playing from a HDD (on a computer made from leftover parts), when high res textures and geometry didn't load fast enough, the engine just showed a low res version before smoothly blending to higher res without ever stuttering. It's a minor miracle how well these games run, even on GPUs 2-3 tiers lower than most others require.
children.textZhHtml: 《毁灭战士》和《毁灭战士：永恒》的性能和流畅性也给我留下了深刻的印象。即使从 HDD（在由剩余部件制成的计算机上）播放时，当高分辨率纹理和几何图形加载速度不够快时，引擎也只会显示低分辨率版本，然后平滑地混合到更高的分辨率，而不会出现卡顿。即使在比大多数其他游戏所需的 GPU 低 2-3 层的情况下，这些游戏的运行效果也能如此出色，这简直就是一个小奇迹。

children.author: midnightclubbed
children.textRawHtml:
id tech is probably the last engine that used a signature game to showcase the engine to licensees. The business model of idTech, Unreal Engine, and Crytech used to be 'make amazing looking game that sells the engine to licensees'. The problem with that model was you got a cut of the game code for whatever the 'amazing looking' game was and told to figure it out. If you wanted to make a Gears of War clone then Unreal Engine 3 was perfect... for every other game genre you had to rewrite and implement huge chunks of code.

Unreal Engine managed to evolve and expand the engine to the point where it is somewhat game agnostic, crytech became lumberyard under Amazon and died, idTech became the engine solely for iD games (ie the same business model as every other game or publisher custom engine).

iD tech's rendering pipeline is focussed on the one AA game iD are publishing that year and making that one game run efficiently and beautifully on mid-high end PC and current consoles. UE5 is focussed on providing tools for anyone to (relatively) easily make good looking games and applications that can be published to a wide range of devices.
children.textZhHtml:
id tech 可能是最后一个使用签名游戏向授权商展示引擎的引擎。 idTech、Unreal Engine 和 Crytech 的商业模式曾经是“制作外观精美的游戏，然后将引擎出售给授权商”。 该模型的问题在于，无论“外观令人惊叹”的游戏是什么，您都会获得游戏代码的一部分，并被告知要弄清楚它。 如果你想制作《战争机器》的克隆版，那么虚幻引擎 3 是完美的...对于你必须重写和实现大量代码的其他游戏类型。

虚幻引擎成功地发展和扩展了引擎，使其在某种程度上与游戏无关，crytech 成为亚马逊旗下的木材场并消亡，idTech 成为专门用于 iD 游戏的引擎（即与所有其他游戏或发行商自定义引擎相同的商业模式）。

iD tech 的渲染管道是专注于 iD 当年发布的一款 AA 游戏，并让这款游戏在中高端 PC 和当前游戏机上高效、美观地运行。 UE5 专注于为任何人提供工具（相对）轻松地制作可发布到各种设备的精美游戏和应用程序。

children.author: danbolt
children.textRawHtml:
I think the big reason they get such good framerates is their conservatism with shader counts. A staff member mentions that in an interview with DigitalFoundry for Doom: The Dark Ages. I wonder if their in-house technology made such restraint easier.

Compared to other engines, making a new material asset is easy for someone less technical to do willy-nilly.
children.textZhHtml:
我认为他们获得如此好的帧率的一个重要原因是他们对着色器计数的保守态度。一位工作人员在接受 DigitalFoundry 的《毁灭战士：黑暗时代》采访时提到了这一点。我想知道他们的内部技术是否使这种限制变得更容易。

与其他引擎相比，制造新的物质资产对于技术水平较低的人来说很容易随心所欲地做。

children.author: midnightclubbed
children.textRawHtml:
For sure, if you restrict what can (and can't) be done in shaders you can optimize buffer layouts and rendering passes, not allow things that explode shader permutations/switching or explode shader instruction count and kill occupancy.

The flip side is the artists loose control and cannot author custom materials.
children.textZhHtml:
当然，如果您限制着色器中可以（和不可以）执行的操作，您可以优化缓冲区布局和渲染通道，不允许发生破坏着色器排列/切换或破坏着色器指令计数并杀死占用的情况。

另一方面是艺术家失去控制，无法创作自定义材质。

children.author: flohofwoe
children.textRawHtml:
> The flip side is the artists loose control and cannot author custom materials.

...which is a good thing tbh. No game needs hundreds or thousands of custom materials.
children.textZhHtml:
> 另一方面是艺术家控制松散，无法创作自定义材料。

...说实话，这是一件好事。没有游戏需要数百或数千种自定义材料。

children.author: cyber_kinetist
children.textRawHtml: I think it can actually be a bit better in terms of artistic consistency to have a fixed set of materials you can work with in a given game. Very important if you want any sort of stylized rendering!
children.textZhHtml: 我认为在艺术一致性方面，拥有一组可以在给定游戏中使用的固定材料实际上会更好一些。如果您想要任何类型的风格化渲染，这一点非常重要！

children.author: WillPostForFood
children.textRawHtml:
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Id_Tech_6#Games_using_id_Tech_...

It may have been great, and lapped the entire industry, but it wasn't getting used very much, even internally at Microsoft. So how much value was really bringing?
children.textZhHtml:
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Id_Tech_6#Games_using_id_Tech_...

它可能很棒，并且席卷了整个行业，但它并没有被广泛使用，甚至在微软内部也是如此。那么真正带来了多少价值？

children.author: canelonesdeverd
children.textRawHtml: "How much value can this bring?" Is the question that the incompetents at microslop needed to ask themselves before absorbing half the industry, not the smug justification for killing great tech after failing to capitalize on it. Specially when it brought great value to the industry for decades.
children.textZhHtml: "这能带来多少价值？"这是 microslop 的无能者在吸收一半行业之前需要问自己的问题，而不是在未能利用伟大技术后扼杀伟大技术的自鸣得意的理由。特别是当它数十年来为行业带来了巨大价值时。

children.author: NooneAtAll3
children.textRawHtml:
> usually because many studios choose to stick close to the rendering defaults, which does lead to a certain look

that's exactly the argument?

you can do anything if you put enough effort in - but effort is finite, so in aggregate the distribution will cluster around the defaults
children.textZhHtml:
> 通常是因为许多工作室选择接近渲染默认值，这确实会导致某种外观

这正是争论的焦点？

如果你付出足够的努力，你可以做任何事情 - 但努力是有限的，所以总的来说，分布将聚集在默认值周围

children.author: superxpro12
children.textRawHtml: I can pick out a creation engine game from a mile away. Engine "Grain" is a real phenomenon. Same for UE5. There's just something about the lighting and the FPS 'feel' that is a dead giveaway.
children.textZhHtml: 我可以从一英里外挑选出一款创作引擎游戏。发动机“颗粒”是一个真实的现象。 UE5 也一样。灯光和 FPS 的“感觉”完全暴露了一切。

children.author: RajT88
children.textRawHtml:
Can you pick it out of films and TV shows?

(Not being snarky - legit question)
children.textZhHtml:
你能从电影和电视节目中选出它吗？

（不是刻薄 - 合法问题）

children.author: maxsilver
children.textRawHtml:
Sometimes yes absolutely.

Netflix shows often have a "house look" to them, because they enforce specific camera requirements and have a standardized / commonly-reused lighting setup -

https://partnerhelp.netflixstudios.com/hc/en-us/articles/360...

https://www.esquire.com/entertainment/tv/a61878509/netflix-s...

---

Marvel movies often reuse a particular pattern of color grading, that can give them a sort of 'similar grain' (see https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hpWYtXtmEFQ )
children.textZhHtml:
有时是的。

Netflix 节目通常具有“家庭外观”，因为它们强制执行特定的摄像机要求并具有标准化/常用的照明设置 -

https://partnerhelp.netflixstudios.com/hc/en-us/articles/360...

https://www.esquire.com/entertainment/tv/a61878509/netflix-s...

---

漫威电影经常重复使用特定的颜色分级模式，这可以给它们一种“相似的纹理”（参见https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hpWYtXtmEFQ）

children.author: dzonga
children.textRawHtml:
yeah Netflix easy to tell & some documentaries.

hell most of these you can even guess the type of camera used even if you're a non pro.
children.textZhHtml:
是的，Netflix 很容易讲述，还有一些纪录片。

大多数内容，即使您不是专业人士，您甚至可以猜出所使用的相机类型。

children.author: wisty
children.textRawHtml: I think they mean the use of UE5 in movies.
children.textZhHtml: 我认为他们指的是UE5在电影中的使用。

children.author: tmtvl
children.textRawHtml: I thought they meant 'can you pick out a Creation Engine game when it appears in a movie or show', though on second thought your idea seems more likely.
children.textZhHtml: 我以为他们的意思是“你能在电影或节目中出现一款创造引擎游戏吗？”，但转念一想，你的想法似乎更有可能。
